sections.paragraph:
(“answering), [Anah], pr.n. m.-

(1) of a son of Seïr, and of the Edomite race sprung from him, Gen 36:20, 29 Gen 36:29.-(2) of a son of Zibeon, and grandson of Seïr, Gen 36:2, 14 Gen 36:14, 24 Gen 36:24 (verses Gen 36:2, 14 Gen 36:14 Anah is called the daughter of Zibeon in the common text; but we should read son (בֵּן), with the Sam. and LXX., as is shewn by verse 24 Gen 36:24. [This change is not necessary; we have only to take בַּת in both its occurrences as in apposition with Aholibamah, the daughter ef Anah, the grand-daughter of Zibeon. See De Rossi]).
